world.title: US's 20-year uranium freeze demand vs Iran's 5: Deadlock that derailed truce talks
world.summary: During recent negotiations in Pakistan, a significant disagreement arose between the United States and Iran regarding Iran's nuclear program. The U.S. proposed a 20-year freeze on uranium enrichment, while Iran countered with a five-year limit. This difference became the main obstacle in the talks, ultimately leading to a deadlock and failure to reach an agreement.

world.title: ‘Apologise for lies’: Trump slams NYT over Iran war coverage, insists Iran ‘obliterated’
world.summary: Donald Trump criticized The New York Times for its coverage of the Iran conflict, asserting it misrepresented Iran's situation as favorable. In a post on Truth Social, he demanded the newspaper apologize for what he termed lies, insisting that Iran had been "obliterated." Trump's comments reflect ongoing tensions regarding media portrayal of international conflicts and U.S.-Iran relations.

world.title: Second Coming: Trump auditions for role after POTUS
world.summary: Former President Donald Trump has engaged in a public dispute with the Catholic Church, following a weekend visit to a cage fight in Miami. This confrontation highlights the ongoing tension between religious institutions and political figures in the U.S. The incident reflects the evolving dynamics of Church and State in contemporary American society, reminiscent of historical conflicts.

business.title: India Inc pulls back a bit from Gulf as cracks widen across operations
business.summary: Indian consumer companies are reducing their operations in the Gulf region due to significant losses and a fivefold increase in container costs linked to the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Expansion plans have been paused as some businesses report declines of up to 40%, with many mass consumers exiting the area, prompting a reevaluation of strategies in West Asia.

business.title: Joint Statement on 13 April by the Heads of the International Energy Agency, International Monetary Fund, and World Bank Group
business.summary: On April 13, the leaders of the International Energy Agency, International Monetary Fund, and World Bank Group issued a joint statement following their meeting in Washington, DC. They discussed their coordination efforts aimed at enhancing institutional responses to energy and environmental challenges. This collaboration, initiated in early April, focuses on maximizing effectiveness in addressing global energy issues.

business.title: "It will reshape the PC landscape": A new rumor says NVIDIA is entering final negotiations to buy a large PC company — Dell and HP stocks jump in the wake, but I'm not so sure
business.summary: NVIDIA is reportedly in final negotiations to acquire a significant company in the PC and server market, potentially reshaping the industry. Following this news, stocks for Dell and HP experienced an increase. Despite NVIDIA's focus on AI GPUs and other priorities, the possibility of this acquisition indicates a strategic shift towards the consumer PC market.

technology.title: Motorola Edge 70 Pro Spotted on Certification Site, India Launch Likely Soon
technology.summary: Motorola is set to launch the Motorola Edge 70 Pro in India soon, as indicated by its recent appearance on a certification website. The device's microsite is currently live on Flipkart, suggesting an imminent release. This development aligns with Motorola's strategy to introduce new smartphones to the Indian market, following recent trends in mobile technology.

technology.title: I finally set up a local coding assistant that works inside my editor - this stack is gold
technology.summary: A developer has successfully set up a local coding assistant that integrates directly into their editor, finding it more efficient than using browser-based tools. While previous experiences with AI assistants like ChatGPT were helpful, subscription costs became burdensome. The local solution alleviates concerns about data privacy and reliance on internet connectivity, enhancing the coding experience significantly.

technology.title: My favorite Pixel AI feature is about to modernize this old-school technology
technology.summary: Google's visual voicemail feature for Pixel phones, Take a Message, is set to receive an upgrade with custom user-recorded greetings. This enhancement will complement its current functionality, which allows users to view incoming voicemails in real-time with live transcriptions. The update aims to modernize the voicemail experience, making it more personalized for users of Pixel smartphones.

sports.title: Praful Hinge makes big revelation: 'I wanted Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's wicket on the first ball'
sports.summary: Praful Hinge made a significant impact in a recent match by taking key wickets from the Rajasthan Royals’ top order, disrupting their run chase. He expressed his desire to dismiss Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the very first ball, showcasing his competitive spirit. Hinge's performance at the Rajiv Gandhi International Stadium highlighted his potential in the tournament.

sports.title: Mitchell Starc, Deepti Sharma named as Wisden's Leading Cricketers in the World
sports.summary: Mitchell Starc and Deepti Sharma have been recognized as Wisden's Leading Cricketers in the World for 2026. In the latest edition of the Wisden Cricketers' Almanack, four Indian players are included among the five Cricketers of the Year. This acknowledgment highlights their exceptional performances and contributions to the sport over the past year.

sports.title: Man Utd player ratings vs Leeds: Leny Yoro and Lisandro Martinez have nightmares as Red Devils suffer shock home defeat despite best efforts of Casemiro & Bruno Fernandes
sports.summary: Manchester United suffered a 2-1 home defeat to Leeds in the Premier League. Player ratings highlighted poor performances from Leny Yoro and Lisandro Martinez, while Casemiro and Bruno Fernandes were noted for their efforts. Goalkeeper Senne Lammens received a 5/10 for failing to communicate effectively with defenders, and Noussair Mazraoui was rated 4/10 for struggles against his opponent.

health.title: Working nights? Study flags rising diabetes, hormonal issues
health.summary: A study in Hyderabad reveals that approximately 77% of night shift workers experience increased insulin resistance, reduced testosterone levels, and unhealthy cholesterol levels compared to day shift workers. Conducted by doctors from Gandhi Medical College, the research highlights potential health risks associated with night shifts, emphasizing the need for awareness and management of these hormonal and metabolic issues.

health.title: Best AR Apps for Education and Learning
health.summary: Augmented reality (AR) apps are transforming education by enhancing classroom experiences and home study. These top-rated AR applications engage students and teachers, moving beyond traditional methods like textbooks and lectures. By incorporating interactive digital materials, AR fosters greater motivation, interest, and comprehension among learners, addressing the limitations of passive learning and promoting a more dynamic educational environment.

science.title: Asia's largest gamma-ray telescope, MACE, explores the high-energy cosmos
science.summary: Asia's largest gamma-ray telescope, MACE, is located 4.3 kilometers above sea level in Ladakh's high-altitude desert. It captures brief flashes of light to investigate extreme cosmic phenomena. Developed by the Bhabha Atomic Research Centre, MACE is the world's third-largest gamma-ray telescope, providing insights into the universe's most violent events and enhancing our understanding of high-energy astrophysics.

science.title: Universe is expanding faster than expected: Scientists struggle to explain cosmic acceleration
science.summary: Scientists have discovered that the Universe is expanding at a rate faster than previously estimated. This acceleration challenges existing theories about cosmic expansion. Researchers have long sought to measure the speed of galaxy movement, but the new findings complicate their understanding of fundamental cosmic forces. Ongoing investigations aim to clarify the mechanisms driving this unexpected acceleration.

science.title: Watch: Artemis II astronaut Christina Koch shares emotional video of reuniting with dog Sadie
science.summary: NASA astronaut Christina Koch shared a heartfelt video of her reunion with her dog, Sadie, after returning from the Artemis II mission. The emotional moment resonated with viewers online, emphasizing the personal aspects of space travel. Koch's return highlights the connection between astronauts and their loved ones, showcasing the impact of long-duration missions on personal relationships.

entertainment.title: ‘Love is not everything’: Actor Trisha’s cryptic reflections on choosing peace spark buzz after Chennai wedding appearance with Vijay
entertainment.summary: Actor Trisha Krishnan's recent cryptic posts about love and peace have generated buzz following her appearance with Vijay at a Chennai wedding. The posts emphasize self-worth during emotionally uncertain times. Their joint appearance has led to widespread online discussion, particularly in light of ongoing rumors regarding Vijay and his wife, Sangeetha Vijay, adding to the intrigue surrounding the situation.

entertainment.title: Toxic: No postponement; makers to roll out a massive promotional campaign
entertainment.summary: Kannada film "Toxic," originally set for a March 19 release, was postponed to June 4 due to the ongoing crisis in the Middle East. Despite earlier speculation about further delays, the filmmakers have confirmed there will be no additional postponement. They plan to launch a significant promotional campaign to support the film's upcoming release.

entertainment.title: 'Army's Puppet': Pakistan's Media Watchdog Faces Flak Over Notice To Channel For Tributes To Asha Bhosle
entertainment.summary: Pakistan's media watchdog, PEMRA, has faced significant criticism after issuing a notice to a channel for airing tributes to singer Asha Bhosle. Journalists and politicians have labeled the action as "cultural insecurity" and a direct attack on the shared heritage of the subcontinent. The backlash highlights ongoing tensions regarding cultural expression and media regulation in the country.
